name: IAgentAdapter
description: Generic port for external agent backend adapters. Defines the structural contract for integrating external agent backends (e.g. Codex, Claude Agent SDK, OpenClaw, A2A) into the workflow engine. Provides a mid-level abstraction for agent-style interactions that may involve tool use, sandboxed execution, and structured output.

methods.name: execute
methods.description: Execute a prompt against the agent backend. Sends a prompt to the external agent and waits for a complete result. The agent may use tools, modify files in a sandbox, and return structured output.

methods.name: stream
methods.description: Stream events from the agent backend. Sends a prompt to the external agent and yields structured event dicts as they arrive. Each event is a dict with at minimum a 'type' key. Useful for real-time progress monitoring and incremental output display.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
execute() must return a complete AgentResult with exit_code (integer), output (string), events (list of dicts), files_changed (list of strings), usage (dict), and agent_id (string)
stream() must yield structured event dicts, not raw text chunks
sandbox parameter must accept one of: 'read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'
When model is null/None, the adapter must fall back to its configured default model
When tools is null/None, the adapter must fall back to its configured default tool set
Implementations must handle agent backend failures gracefully and surface them in AgentResult
